title: 2030 Alps Games already facing storm of internal controversies and public backlash
subhed: France received the Olympic flag on Sunday during the closing ceremony of the Milan-Cortina Winter Games. Behind the scenes, however, the handover has been anything but smooth, with multiple crises brewing and public resistance on the rise.

title: Top Mexican drug cartel leader killed
Understanding the battle of Verdun through rare or little-known photographs
subhed: A book published to mark the 110th anniversary of the legendary battle brings to light many images taken by army photographers. Le Monde is presenting several of these photographs, offering a glimpse into the reality behind this symbol of the First World War.

title: Donald Trump's support with 'bro culture' is waning
subhed: With his racist post about the Obamas, support for immigration police and decision to politicize Puerto Rican singer Bad Bunny's halftime Super Bowl show, the US president has alienated some of his supporters.

title: Hong Kong appeals court upholds prison sentences for 12 pro-democracy activists
subhed: The Hong Kong opposition figures had their appeals dismissed, with their sentences ranging from four years and two months to 10 years, in a ruling that highlights the severity of Beijing's control over Hong Kong's politics.

title: Ukraine's Western allies push to drag the war out
subhed: All while sending weapons and declaring their support for Kyiv, Europeans and Americans have been tempted to play for time rather than push for a ceasefire. A truce would force them to deploy ground troops, and so they continue to rely on a war of attrition, leaving the Ukrainians on the front lines against the Russians.
byline: By Chloé Hoorman and Elise Vincent

title: Karyna Shuliak was Jeffrey Epstein's last partner and main heiress. She won't benefit from his will
subhed: The Belarusian woman met the sexual predator when she was 20 and remained loyal to him until his suicide in prison in 2019.

title: How a messianic movement is changing the Israeli military
subhed: Followers of the Hardal movement, a blend of religious fundamentalism and ultranationalism, have begun to make inroads into the military establishment. Their dehumanizing rhetoric toward Palestinians has contributed to the destruction of the Gaza Strip.

title: Oleksandr Syrsky, commander of the Ukrainian army, to Le Monde: 'Russian army losses have exceeded its recruitment level'
subhed: The Ukrainian commander-in-chief told Le Monde that, despite a 'very difficult' situation on the battlefield, his army continued to resist 'the most powerful army in Europe' and to 'foil' Russian military plans.
